text:
Once upon a time in a little village nestled in greenleaf woods.

There lived a boy named darwin smith. He loved beetles. So obsessed.

One summer. Darwy knew birds.

One afternoon he found cicadas.

Now cicadas love trees.

That's where Darwy saw a branch.

He tapped.

A cicada buzz.

It talked.

You fly?

You talk?

Well my wings...

They didn't work.

Later Darwy built clubs.

He knew he could change worlds.




With every letter.

It got big.

Soon towns.

And cities.

People were quiet.

At night birds sang.

They stopped. Fade.

And nothing sounded.

Except Darwy.




I fly?

How?

They sing.

Darwin just smiled.




It grows.

Days.

Into summers.

And winters.

It goes. Sing.

Sing stars?

Maybe.



But one summer.

A voice.

A red robin.

Just said.


So darwy listened.



Just think.

Maybe I could do that.

Maybe fly
